{{Quote|I used to run into them occasionally on out-of-the-way worlds in the CorpSec—Ession, Ninn, Matra VI.|The smuggler Roa identifies members of the Ryn species, which he had previously encountered on Ninn|<ref name="AoC">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref>}}
'''Ninn''' was a small [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Ninn system/Legends|Ninn system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The world was despoiled and depopulated in an ancient conflict, but was later resettled by a group that became known as the [[Priests of Ninn]]. Ninn came to be part of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] following that region's expansion, and so fell under the control of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]].

==Description==
Ninn was a small [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA" /> located in the [[Ninn system/Legends|Ninn system]]<ref name="EAOC" /> of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Tingel Arm/Legends|Tingel Arm]] region.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
During an ancient conflict, the world was depopulated and despoiled. Following the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="TEA" /> after its [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Ninn was one of many planets included in the newly-expanded Corporate Sector. Its inclusion in the Corporate [[Sector/Legends|Sector]] meant that it fell under the control of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the controlling body of the sector. The Ninn [[Star system/Legends|star system]] came to be part of the [[Authority Guardian Corridor]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] and another [[Lo'Uran-Lythos hyperlane|hyperlane]] that connected it to the [[Lo'Uran system/Legends|Lo'Uran system]] and the [[Lythos system/Legends|Lythos system]].<ref name="TEA" />

By around [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> the planet had been settled by a group that became known as the [[Priests of Ninn]]. The priests lived in a planet-wide refuge, where they practiced their beliefs, including formalistic absolution.<ref name="GTTSWG" /> Members of the nomadic [[Ryn/Legends|Ryn]] [[Species/Legends|species]] also sometimes visited the world, and the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Roa]] encountered some there at one point in his career.<ref name="AoC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ninn was first mentioned in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', a novel released in [[1979]] and written by [[Brian Daley]].<ref name="HSR">''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''</ref> It was identified as a planet in the entry for the Priests of Ninn in the first edition of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'', which was released in [[1984]] and compiled by [[Raymond L. Velasco]].<ref name="GTTSWG" />
